## Root Cause Summary

Issue: Applying a 0W power limit policy on the MemorySubsystem domain (D2) via NM Redfish schema has no effect. Power values remain at workload-max (~70W) instead of dropping to the floor/min capability value. This is a regression — D2 power limiting worked correctly in BKC 12.

Last Known Good: BKC 12

First Known Bad: BKC 14

Other domains (D0, D1, D5): Working as expected — not affected.

Reproduction Steps:

Run stress-ng workload (CPU + MEM, on X4 system)

Read MemorySubsystem (D2) power st

### Forum Notes
log is ready, Primecode is working on it.

### Description
Issue: Applying a 0W power limit policy on the MemorySubsystem domain (D2) via NM Redfish schema has no effect. Power values remain at workload-max (~70W) instead of dropping to the floor/min capability value. This is a regression — D2 power limiting worked correctly in BKC 12.

Last Known Good: BKC 12

First Known Bad: BKC 14

Other domains (D0, D1, D5): Working as expected — not affected.

Reproduction Steps:

Run stress-ng workload (CPU + MEM, on X4 system)

Read MemorySubsystem (D2) power statistics → ~70W observed

Create 0W policy for D2 domain using NM Redfish schema

Read D2 power → still ~70W (expected: drop to floor/min capability)

Delete the 0W policy

Read D2 power → still ~70W (expected: uncapped but at baseline min)

Expected
: Power should drop to floor value when 0W policy is active; after deletion, should reflect uncapped minimum.

Actual: Power remains at ~70W throughout — NM policy has zero effect on D2.

### Comments (latest)
++++22611914290 agraback
Does the 0W Memory Subsystem Policy mean a 0W DRAM RAPL limit applied by csr/tpmi ? The BKC14 behavior in the description shows the power going down to ~70W but not able to recover, right? And then BKC 20 behavior has changed to show the power going down further to ~30W? Is this one able to recover after removing the 0W limit? If suspecting primecode, please include the imh pcudata log dump. sv.socket0.imhs.pcudata(r"path/to/pcudata_both_imhs.txt")
++++1667516343 psingh2
please find log attached for pcudata_both_imhs : https://hsdes.intel.com/resource/16030622164 Host: cs16ca101dn0503 (1S X4)  BKC20  @Grabacki, Alex  Yes with BKC20, power is constrained to 30-40W under active workloads(stress-ng+mlc). Creating or deleting a 0W (0 power cap) NM policy has no effect - power remains unchanged
